sections.paragraph:
εὔτεκνος, ον (s. τέκνον; Eur. et al.; Philo). In ISm 13:2 the word is understood by Zahn and Hilgenfeld as an adj., in which case any of the var. mngs. found in other lit. is applicable: with many children, w. good children, fortunate because of children. Other scholars, including Lghtf., Funk, Krüger, Bihlmeyer, treat εὔ. as a proper name, because εὔ. as an adj. seems not to fit well into the context. On the other hand, Εὔ. as a name is unattested to date; but s. OGI 53 and the grammarian Εὐτέκνιος in Pauly-W. VI 1492.—DELG s.v. τίκτω.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

adj. adj. = adjective

var. var. = various(ly)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

Funk Funk , s. B-D-F—List 6

Bihlmeyer Bihlmeyer = KB., Die Apostolischen Väter—List 6

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 414
